purpose: Define the fixed protocol chain that turns agent work, prompt surfaces, orchestration, architecture, shell execution, human and agent collaboration, DTP packets, and governance verdicts into one auditable release path.

stages.gate_rule: agent outputs must not enter release review without handbook acknowledgement and prompt bundle linkage
prompt bundle must reference handbook, orchestration, and architecture inputs before execution planning
orchestration must remain reviewable and hash-linked to the handbook, intake workflow, and prompt bundle
architecture approval must exist before shell execution is considered valid
shell execution must not proceed without architecture, approval, and rollback context
human and agent ideation must be trace-linked before governance verdicts are issued
protocol packets must remain append-only and hash-linked
governance verdicts are invalid unless upstream refs are complete and hash-linked

invariants: Agent entry must begin from the service handbook and handbook acknowledgement packet.
Agent outputs must not enter release review without prompt bundle references.
Service intake workflow packets must exist before orchestration is considered valid.
Prompt bundles must not flow into execution without orchestration and architecture references.
Shell execution must not run without architecture, approval, and rollback context.
Human and agent ideation must be stored as DTP-linked collaboration packets.
Governance verdicts must not be valid without upstream hash-linked protocol refs.
